en.text: that had been the wife of Urias
: Josias: some read, Josias begat Jakim, and Jakim begat Jechonias
. they: or, his name shall be called
: meet: or, answerable to amendment of life
. tempt: or, try, or, put to trial, or, proof
those which were possessed with devils
. a bushel: the word in the original signifieth a measure containing about a pint less than a peck
. offend: or, do cause thee to offend
. alms: or, righteousness of your: or, with your
. do not: or, cause not a trumpet to be sounded
what was befallen to the possessed of the devils
a man sick of the palsy
unto the sick of the palsy
to the sick of the palsy
that which is put in to fill it up
. bottles: or, sacks of skin, or, leather
, which was diseased with an issue of blood
. fainted: or, were tired and lay down
shall they call them of his
. farthing: it is in value halfpenny farthing in the original, as being the tenth part of the Roman penny
shall be they of his own
have the gospel preached to them
. suffereth: or, is gotten by force, and they that thrust men
. offended: he relapseth, or, falleth into sin
. measures: the word in the Greek is a measure containing about a peck and a half, wanting a little more than a pint
things which have been kept secret
, tetrarch: or, governor over four provinces
. do: or, are wrought by him
them which sat with him at meat
. Peter: this name signifies a rock
? tribute: called in the original, didrachma, being in value fifteen pence sterling; about thirty seven cents
. a piece: or, a stater: it is half an ounce of silver, in value two shillings and six pence, sterling; about fifty five cents
. talents: a talent is 750.ounces of silver, which after five shillings the ounce is 187.li. 10.s.
, and took him by the throat
. pence: the Roman penny is the eighth part of an ounce, which after five shillings the ounce is seven pence halfpenny; about fourteen cents
. penny: the Roman penny is the eighth part of an ounce, which after five shillings the ounce is seven pence halfpenny; about fourteen cents
. have wrought: or, have continued one hour only
it shall be given to them for whom
it, they were moved with indignation
. a fig tree: Gr. one fig tree
which is done to the fig tree
. penny: in value seven pence halfpenny
, when he had married a wife
, phylacteries: pieces of parchment containing sentences of the law
. with: or, with a trumpet, and a great voice
the kingdom of heaven is as
. talents: a talent is 187.pounds 10.ten shillings, chap.18.24
day of the feast of unleavened bread
. blessed it: many Greek copies have gave thanks
when they had sung an hymn
. offended: or, though the faith of other men should be shaken and fail, yet mine will be firm and constant
others smote him with the palms of their hands
; whom: or, whom they bought of the children of Israel
of soldiers. common hall: or, governor's house
: teach: or, make disciples, or, Christians of all nations
